hero.title: How to Measure
hero.subtitle: Accurate measurements are the foundation of custom-fit window treatments. Use our technical guides to determine the correct dimensions for your hardware and space.

productTypes.title: Select Product Category
productTypes.subtitle: Different window treatments require specific measurement logic. Choose the guide that matches your project requirements.

products.title: Curtains & Drapes
products.description: The 'Point-to-Point' measurement method: provide dimensions from the top of your hardware to your desired endpoint. Fullness is calculated at the workroom level.

products.title: Woven & Roman Shades
products.description: The 'Opening-First' method: provide the raw window dimensions. All necessary factory deductions for inside mounts are handled during production.

curtainsGuide.title: Drapery Measurement Guide
curtainsGuide.subtitle: Technical steps for determining finished width and height.

steps.title: Determine Coverage Width
steps.description: Measure the full width of your rod or track. If hardware is not yet installed, measure the window and add 4-8" per side for optimal coverage. You can add even more allowance if you want to create the effect of a wider window. Note: Drapes are sold as Single Panels; for a pair, divide the total width by two.

tip.label: Design Note:
tip.text: Extended width allows panels to stack off-glass when open, maximizing natural light and creating a grander architectural feel.

steps.title: Measure Finished Height
steps.description: Measure vertically from the top of the rod or track to the exact point where you want the fabric to end. This value will be the final panel length produced.
items: Measure from the top of the hardware
Consistent logic for Grommet and Pleated styles

steps.title: Select Floor Clearance
steps.description: Determine the final measurement based on your preferred aesthetic relationship with the floor:

steps.title: Order Specifications
steps.description: Enter the width for one individual panel. The workroom automatically applies the appropriate fullness (typically 2.0x to 2.2x) to the width provided.

shadesGuide.title: Shades Measurement Guide
shadesGuide.subtitle: Provide your exact window opening dimensions. We handle the deductions.

steps.title: Check Mounting Depth
steps.description: Measure the window frame depth. Inside mount Roman or Woven shades typically require a minimum of 2.5" of flat surface for a flush fit.

warning.label: Requirement:
warning.text: If depth is shallow, choose Outside Mount for a bold look.

steps.title: Measure Width (Inside vs Outside)
steps.insideMount: Measure the opening at the top, middle, and bottom. Record the narrowest width. Round down to the nearest 1/8". We take the factory deduction.
steps.outsideMount: Measure the total area to be covered. We recommend adding 2" to each side (4" total) for optimal light blockage.

steps.title: Measure Height (Inside vs Outside)
steps.insideMount: Measure at the left, center, and right. Record the longest height. Round up to the nearest 1/8".
steps.outsideMount: Measure the full area from the top of the headrail to your desired bottom endpoint. No deductions will be taken.

steps.title: Visual Consistency
steps.description: If ordering multiple shades for the same room, use the largest height measurement across all windows to ensure identical pleat alignment.

commonMistakes.title: Professional Standards
commonMistakes.subtitle: Key considerations for ensuring technical accuracy in your custom order.

mistakes.title: Panel Quantity Logic
mistakes.description: Drapes are crafted as single units. If you require a pair to cover a single rod, ensure the width entered is half of the total coverage and the quantity is set to two.

mistakes.title: Measurement Tools
mistakes.description: Always use a professional steel tape measure. Fabric or plastic tapes can stretch, leading to inaccurate results at the 1/8" level.

mistakes.title: Hardware Starting Point
mistakes.description: Height measurements must begin from the top of the hardware. Starting from the window frame or sill will result in panels that are too short.

mistakes.title: Manual Deduction Errors
mistakes.description: For inside-mount shades, do not subtract any width yourself. Providing pre-deducted measurements may cause gaps that exceed our light-control standards.

needHelp.title: Concierge Support
needHelp.description: Our trade specialists can review your project photos or schedule a video call to verify your dimensions before production begins.
